citation_short: Main Statistical Office in Chernihiv Region, 2022 population table
title: Чисельність населення (за оцінкою) на 1 січня 2022 року
author_or_org: Main Statistical Office in Chernihiv Region
strengths: Official pre-war resident-population baseline for Chernihiv city.
limitations: Stock only; does not adjust for wartime displacement or temporary absence.
notes: Search snippet shows Chernihiv city resident population at 282,747 as of 1 January 2022.

title: Ukraine: Civilians in Besieged Chernihiv Need Access to Essentials
strengths: City-bounded and explicit about both remaining population and a civilian-death lower bound.
limitations: Snapshot taken during the siege; civilian death count is rough and clearly incomplete.
notes: Reports approximately 130,000 residents remaining and more than 350 civilians killed.

title: Chernihiv: About 700 people were killed during the blockade of the city by Russian troops – the mayor
author_or_org: Ukrainska Pravda (quoting Mayor Vladyslav Atroshenko / Ukrinform)
strengths: City-specific and explicit about total deaths and an unidentified subset.
limitations: Second-hand quote; inclusion criteria are not published; mixes military and civilian deaths; uses approximate wording.
notes: Used only as contextual input to a conservative residual military-death estimate.

title: Spotlight Brief No. 2, 2022: Operational Overview of the Russian Invasion of Ukraine
geographic_scope: Northern and eastern fronts of the invasion, including Kyiv/Chernihiv/Kharkiv
strengths: Explicit front-level force assignment and siege timing for Kyiv, Chernihiv, and Kharkiv sectors.
limitations: Not a casualty dataset and not a city-only force-strength time series.
notes: Used to anchor theater boundaries and force packages rather than to supply deaths.

title: Preliminary Lessons in Conventional Warfighting from Russia’s Invasion of Ukraine: February–July 2022
geographic_scope: Ukraine, with specific discussion of Kyiv/Chernihiv axis
strengths: Explains how Ukrainian defenders were reinforced by reservists and territorial defence.
limitations: Not a casualty dataset; some underlying operational data remain non-public.
notes: Used to justify modeled Chernihiv defender person-month scenarios.

title: Thematic Report on Killings of Civilians in Parts of Kyiv, Chernihiv, and Sumy Regions from 24 February to 6 April 2022
author_or_org: Office of the High Commissioner for Human Rights / Human Rights Monitoring Mission in Ukraine
geographic_scope: Parts of Kyiv, Chernihiv and Sumy regions under Russian control
strengths: Strong side attribution and clear date range for occupation-killing window.
limitations: Aggregates three regions; not a clean standalone total for the Kyiv northwest cluster alone.
notes: Key source for Bucha occupation timing and Russian-force attribution.

title: Amnesty says Russia guilty of war crimes in Kharkiv shelling
date_scope: from 2022-02-24 until Russians were pushed back in May 2022
strengths: Compact city-bounded civilian-death and evacuation figures with a clear temporal frame.
limitations: Reuters is reporting Ukrainian figures rather than publishing a named-victim dataset.
notes: Useful floor for city-level civilian direct deaths.

title: Mayor of Ukraine's Kharkiv says city is under intense bombardment
strengths: Direct mayoral estimate of remaining population during bombardment.
limitations: Snapshot estimate; not a daily time series.
notes: States around 1 million remained and about 30% had evacuated.

title: Intelligence confirms large-scale losses of Russia’s 1st Tank Army in Ukraine
geographic_scope: Russian 1st Guards Tank Army in the Kharkiv theater
strengths: Explicitly separates killed, wounded, and total casualties for a named Russian formation.
limitations: Partial formation-specific claim, not an episode total; reported through Ukrainian intelligence channels.
notes: Useful as a lower-bound partial military-loss datapoint, not as a full Kharkiv battle-death estimate.

title: “Our City Was Gone”: Russia’s Devastation of Mariupol, Ukraine
author_or_org: Human Rights Watch, Truth Hounds, and SITU Research
strengths: Transparent methods and explicit limitations; excellent for separating what can and cannot be inferred from burial counts.
limitations: Burials are not a clean direct-death series and do not separate civilians from military personnel.
notes: Critical for explaining why Mariupol should not be over-claimed.

title: High Commissioner Updates the Human Rights Council on Mariupol, Ukraine
author_or_org: Office of the High Commissioner for Human Rights
strengths: Clear direct-hostilities framing and explicit child subset.
limitations: Verified floor only; OHCHR also warns the actual civilian death toll is likely much higher.
notes: Best conservative direct-civilian floor for Mariupol in this bundle.

title: Timeline: Russia's siege of the Ukrainian city of Mariupol
strengths: Useful contemporary snapshot of trapped population and local-official death claims.
limitations: Not a forensic death count; local estimates were highly contested during the siege.
notes: Used as contextual evidence only, not in the core direct-death path.

title: Russia to receive people fleeing Russian-held parts of Ukraine's Kherson
geographic_scope: Kherson city and occupied parts of Kherson region
strengths: Useful displacement context showing how quickly the west-bank civilian denominator had already shifted.
limitations: Does not provide a clean west-bank headcount or direct civilian death total.
notes: Reports that hundreds of thousands had fled, including half the pre-war population of Kherson city.

title: Russia tells people in occupied Kherson to 'save your lives' and leave
geographic_scope: Western bank of the Dnipro in occupied Kherson region
strengths: Explicitly west-bank and therefore more aligned to the intended episode footprint.
limitations: Reported evacuation target rather than verified physically present population.
notes: States Russia's proxies were trying to evacuate up to 60,000 people on the western bank.

title: Explainer: Why Ukraine's southern Kherson region is a strategic prize
strengths: Useful baseline reminder that the relevant episode is subregional, not all-Kherson-oblast by default.
limitations: Region total only; not suitable by itself for the west-bank campaign denominator.
notes: Reports the region's pre-war population as more than 1 million.

title: Preliminary Lessons from Ukraine’s Offensive Operations, 2022–23
strengths: Provides a bounded Bakhmut-pocket casualty statement from a serious operational study.
limitations: Combines killed and severely wounded; not separable into deaths only.
notes: Useful mainly to explain why Bakhmut is still not ready for a full DR.
